chapter_title.en: Chapter on Aqiqah, Tahnik, Naming, Kunyah, Shaving the newborn's Head, Piercing the Ears, and Circumcision

text: 4710 - رَوَى عُمَرُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ: "كُلُّ اِمْرِئٍ مُرْتَهَنٌ، يَوْمَ اَلْقِيَامَةِ بِعَقِيقَتِهِ وَ اَلْعَقِيقَةُ أَوْجَبُ مِنَ اَلْأُضْحِيَّةِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.4710 - Umar ibn Yazid narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) who said: "Every person is held in pledge on the Day of Judgment by their Aqiqah, and the Aqiqah is more obligatory than the sacrificial offering (Udhiyyah)."
